title: Pi bridge opens Herdr cockpit
goal: Reduce the Khazad-Doom Pi extension to the jobs Pi is good at: starting/shaping/explaining workflows, answering blockers, summarizing handoffs, and opening or focusing the Herdr cockpit; remove any expectation that Pi emulates a full live multi-agent dashboard.
acceptance: The Pi adapter surface is documented and implemented as a thin bridge: start or shape a run, explain current run state from daemon feed/handoff data, answer operator blockers through daemon commands, summarize handoff, and open or focus the Herdr cockpit for a run.
The Pi adapter does not render or emulate a full live multi-agent cockpit, does not own process/session lifecycle, and does not infer workflow state from Pi session lifecycle.
An explicit command or adapter action can open/focus the Herdr workspace for the latest or specified run and reports a clear fallback/remediation message when Herdr is unavailable.
All live status text shown through Pi comes from the daemon-owned feed projection added by FEED-01; no raw event-type interpretation is duplicated in the Pi adapter.
README, package metadata, and skill docs present the intended model: Pi starts/shapes/explains, Khazad-Doom owns run truth, Herdr shows and focuses live agents.
Existing operator escalation behavior remains intact: answering blockers goes through daemon state and token-scoped worker questions, not through manual typing in Herdr worker panes.
must_ask_if: The implementation would restore a rich Pi monitor overlay or make Pi a peer cockpit to Herdr.
The implementation would let Pi or Herdr mutate run truth directly instead of calling daemon commands/interfaces.
The implementation would duplicate status/progress interpretation in JavaScript instead of consuming the daemon feed projection.
The implementation would require Herdr for blocker answers, handoff summaries, or headless CLI operation.
The implementation would remove existing explicit CLI status/watch/monitor fallback surfaces.
